# Sisal

> Sisal is a JSR-first, Deno-native database toolkit with npm support for typed schemas, safe SQL, query builders, migration planning, and small adapter packages.

Workspace package version: 0.12.0. The core ORM is driverless; adapters for PostgreSQL, Neon, SQLite, libSQL/Turso, and MySQL/MariaDB live at explicit package boundaries.

Use `llms-full.txt` when you need a focused single-file package/API reference with links into the canonical documentation.

## Primary Documentation

- [Focused LLM Context](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/llms-full.txt): Generated focused context from package manifests, exported API docs, and a canonical documentation map.
- [API Reference](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/api.html): This reference is verified against the current workspace package exports and source layout. Sisal is a JSR-first, Deno-native database toolkit that is also published to npm for Node.js 24+. It has a driverless core,...
- [Migration Notes](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/migration-notes.html): Sisal replaces the earlier ORM and migration package direction with a focused database toolkit.
- [Drizzle Parity](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/drizzle-parity.html): This is the guiding document for how Sisal's API evolves. It maps every relevant part of the Drizzle ORM 0.45.2 surface to Sisal, records where we intentionally diverge, and lays out the roadmap to close the gaps...
- [PostgreSQL compatibility](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/pg-compatibility.html): Sisal's PostgreSQL adapter (@sisal/pg) is verified end-to-end against a real server on every supported major version. The suite connects with the default driver — postgres.js (npm:postgres) since v0.10 (CF1) —...
- [Neon compatibility](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/neon-compatibility.html): Sisal's Neon adapter (@sisal/neon) targets Neon serverless PostgreSQL. It speaks the Postgres wire protocol over a WebSocket via jsr:@neon/serverless and **reuses the Postgres SQL dialect and DDL**, so its feature...
- [SQLite compatibility](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/sqlite-compatibility.html): Sisal's SQLite adapter (@sisal/sqlite) is verified end-to-end against a real, embedded SQLite database. The suite opens a temp file with the bundled jsr:@db/sqlite driver, applies generated DDL, and exercises every...
- [libSQL / Turso compatibility](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/libsql-compatibility.html): Sisal's libSQL adapter (@sisal/libsql) is verified end-to-end against a real libSQL database. libSQL is a SQLite fork, so the SQL surface matches SQLite; what differs is the **connection** — a local file:, an...
- [Benchmarks](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/benchmarks.html): Sisal benchmarks measure the work the **library** does — building SQL, rendering it, and turning driver rows into typed results — in isolation from the database and the network. A query's wall-clock time is dominated...
- [Security](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/security.html): This is the single source of truth for Sisal's security posture: a **code-level audit**, a **living roadmap** of addressed and open concerns, and the **invariants pinned by tests** so the posture cannot silently...
- [Analytics Readiness](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/analytics-readiness.html): **Date:** 2026-07-01 · **Status:** A1-A4 🟢 · **Scope:** investigation and type prototype only. No @sisal/analytics package, renderer, runtime, dashboard helper, public export, or generated feature-matrix claim ships...
- [Package Architecture](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/architecture.html): Sisal is, and will remain, a **JSR-first, Deno-native, SQL-first, type-safe** toolkit for **relational** data access, with matching npm packages for Node.js 24+. It is not becoming an object-first ORM. As the surface...
- [@sisal/core — the compile-target contract](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/core-ir.html): The documented, versioned public surface downstream packages (@sisal/etl, @sisal/analytics) compile into — the v0.8 item-3 stamp. What this page declares public is a compatibility commitment; everything else is not.
- [@sisal/core extraction — migration notes](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/core-migration.html): v0.8 extracted @sisal/orm's lower tier into **@sisal/core** (roadmap item 2): the schema primitives, fragment SQL IR, expression operators, the dialect capability registry, and the dialect-aware renderer. The fluent...
- [Editor LSP Setup](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/editor-lsp.html): Sisal is a Deno workspace, so editors should attach TypeScript files to deno lsp, not to a plain Node/TypeScript language server. A generic LSP client is fine as long as the server process is Deno's language server.
- [ETL pain points](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/etl-pain-points.html): What actually happens when a @sisal/etl (v0.10 preview) deployment breaks. Every entry is pinned by a live-PostgreSQL scenario in integration/etl_limits_test.ts (run alongside the acceptance suite...
- [ETL scheduling](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/etl-scheduling.html): Sisal ETL is deliberately **not** a scheduler (v0.10 non-goals). The division of labor:
- [Feature matrix](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/feature-matrix.html): Source of truth: tools/feature_matrix.ts Regenerate: deno task docs:matrix · Verify: deno task docs:matrix:check -->
- [MySQL / MariaDB compatibility](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/mysql-compatibility.html): Sisal's MySQL adapter (@sisal/mysql) is verified end-to-end against real servers — **both** MySQL proper and MariaDB, which share one adapter but are distinct capability profiles. Each suite opens a pool with the...
- [MySQL type & DDL mapping](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/mysql-ddl-mapping.html): **Date:** 2026-07-01 · **Probe:** perf/mysql_ddl_probe.ts (deno task perf:mysql:ddl) · **Verified against:** MySQL **8.4.10** and MariaDB **11.8.8** (Docker), decoded through the C6-chosen driver (npm:mysql2 with the...
- [MySQL readiness](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/mysql-readiness.html): **Date:** 2026-07-01 · **Status:** all six C tasks 🟢 · **Verified against:** MySQL **8.4.10** and MariaDB **11.8.8** (Docker), Deno 2.9.0 (+ Node 26 dual-runtime check)
- [Use Sisal from Node](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/node.html): Sisal is JSR-first and Deno-native, and the same package set is published to **npm** under the **@sisaljs** scope for **Node.js 24+**. The API is identical to the JSR packages; the install command, import scope, and...
- [npm Distribution Plan](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/npm-distribution-plan.html): This is the **actionable execution plan and maintenance record** for publishing Sisal to **npm** under the @sisaljs scope, running on **Node.js 24+**, alongside the JSR distribution. It is the "how / tasks" companion...
- [npm Release Readiness](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/npm-release-readiness.html): This began as a **standing readiness report** for what would need to change for Sisal to publish to **npm** alongside JSR. As of v0.12, that distribution track has shipped: Sisal remains JSR-first and Deno-native,...
- [npm Release Runbook](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/npm-release-runbook.html): How a paired **JSR + npm** release of Sisal is cut. The Publish workflow (.github/workflows/publish.yml) does the work; this is the operator checklist. Companion to npm-distribution-plan.md (Phase 7).
- [Portability policies](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/portability-policies.html): The cross-family value and lifecycle policies Sisal's examples, adapters, and tests follow (v0.8 item 17). Per-engine details live in the docs/*-compatibility.md pages; this page states the policies once.
- [Roadmap Sequencing Audit (v0.6 → v0.11)](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/roadmap-sequencing-audit.html): This is a **second-pass sequencing audit** of the forward plan (docs/roadmap.md, docs/architecture.md, docs/v0.6.0-roadmap.md → docs/v0.11.0-roadmap.md, and the twelve examples/advanced-sql-contracts/). It...
- [Roadmap Overview](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/roadmap.html): Sisal is a **JSR-first, Deno-native, SQL-first, type-safe** ORM and database toolkit for **relational** data across PostgreSQL, Neon, SQLite, libSQL, and MySQL/MariaDB. As of v0.12, the same package set is also...
- [v0.10.0 Roadmap](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/v0.10.0-roadmap.html): The investigations (v0.6) and substrate (v0.8, v0.9) are done; v0.10 ships the **first preview of Sisal ETL** as a separate package. The model is deliberately small: a **job definition + a single-run runner** that...
- [v0.11.0 Roadmap](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/v0.11.0-roadmap.html): With the analytical IR designed (v0.7) and the core IR stabilized (v0.8), v0.11 ships the first **Postgres-first analytical query layer** — a typed OLAP API that queries rollups and analytical tables using Sisal...
- [v0.4.0 Roadmap](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/v0.4.0-roadmap.html): This document captures the API gaps surfaced while building the examples/neon-hot-feed example — a Reddit-style hot feed on Neon/PostgreSQL that must stay Deno Deploy / Neon-HTTP friendly. Each gap forced the example...
- [v0.5.0 Roadmap](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/v0.5.0-roadmap.html): Where v0.4.0 closed _feature_ gaps surfaced by the neon-hot-feed example, v0.5.0 closes _parity_ gaps **between the adapters**.
- [v0.6.0 Roadmap](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/v0.6.0-roadmap.html): Where v0.5.0 makes the OLTP builder boringly correct across the four dialects, v0.6.0 is the **readiness** milestone: it earns the right to build later packages (and reach a fifth dialect) by proving the foundation...
- [v0.7.0 Roadmap](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/v0.7.0-roadmap.html): v0.7 carries **two parallel workstreams**:
- [v0.8.0 Roadmap](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/v0.8.0-roadmap.html): v0.6 and v0.7 are investigations; v0.8 is where their findings become a **stable substrate**. The goal is to stabilize Sisal's internal SQL representation and expression system — and extract **@sisal/core** — so that...
- [v0.9.0 Roadmap](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/v0.9.0-roadmap.html): With the IR stabilized in v0.8 and **MySQL added in v0.7**, v0.9 hardens the **five** adapters against the ETL/analytics SQL shapes surfaced by v0.6/v0.7, and turns the implicit, scattered dialect knowledge into an...

## Package APIs

- [@sisal/core](https://jsr.io/@sisal/core): The driverless foundation of Sisal: schema primitives, a fragment SQL IR, expression operators, the capability registry, and the dialect-aware renderer every other package builds on. Exports: ., ./schema, ./unstable-internal.
- [@sisal/orm](https://jsr.io/@sisal/orm): Sisal's driver-neutral ORM: type-safe schemas, safe SQL expressions, immutable query builders, relations, a typed function caller, and serializable schema snapshots. Exports: ., ./core, ./error, ./logger, ./schema.
- [@sisal/migrate](https://jsr.io/@sisal/migrate): Schema migrations for Sisal: snapshot diffing, migration planning, additive DDL generation, checksums, and a driverless runner with drift detection. Exports: ., ./cli, ./core, ./workflow.
- [@sisal/etl](https://jsr.io/@sisal/etl): SQL-pushdown ETL for Sisal: typed, checkpointed rollup jobs that aggregate straight in the database, one window at a time. Exports: ..
- [@sisal/analytics](https://jsr.io/@sisal/analytics): Typed analytical queries for Sisal: compose metrics × dimensions × time windows over rollup tables. Postgres-first. Exports: ..
- [@sisal/pg](https://jsr.io/@sisal/pg): PostgreSQL adapter for Sisal: typed ORM execution, dialect-aware SQL rendering, connection pooling, and migrations. Exports: ., ./ddl, ./migrate, ./orm.
- [@sisal/sqlite](https://jsr.io/@sisal/sqlite): SQLite adapter for Sisal: embedded, typed ORM execution with dialect-aware SQL rendering and migrations. Exports: ., ./ddl, ./migrate, ./orm.
- [@sisal/libsql](https://jsr.io/@sisal/libsql): libSQL & Turso adapter for Sisal: typed ORM execution over local files, remote Turso, or embedded replicas, with migrations. Exports: ., ./ddl, ./migrate, ./orm.
- [@sisal/mysql](https://jsr.io/@sisal/mysql): MySQL & MariaDB adapter for Sisal: one adapter with engine-detected, typed ORM execution, SQL rendering, and migrations. Exports: ., ./ddl, ./migrate, ./orm.
- [@sisal/neon](https://jsr.io/@sisal/neon): Neon serverless PostgreSQL adapter for Sisal: typed ORM execution over a WebSocket pool, plus SQL rendering and migrations. Exports: ., ./ddl, ./migrate, ./orm.

## Project Context

- [Sisal](https://github.com/gilvandovieira/sisal/blob/main/README.md): Sisal is a JSR-first, Deno-native collection of **ORM and query-builder packages**, also published to npm for Node.js 24+ as of v0.12 — typed schemas, safe SQL builders, schema snapshots, migration planning, and...
- [Changelog](https://github.com/gilvandovieira/sisal/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md): This is the canonical changelog for Sisal workspace releases.
- [Repository](https://github.com/gilvandovieira/sisal): Source code, issues, examples, workflows, and package directories.
